ogTitle: South Townsville vs South Wellesley Islands: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Townsville and South Wellesley Islands.
heading: South Townsville vs South Wellesley Islands — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Townsville, Queensland and South Wellesley Islands, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Wellesley Islands has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to South Townsville (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Townsville or South Wellesley Islands safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Townsville has a flood risk score of 49/100, while South Wellesley Islands scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Townsville scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. South Wellesley Islands scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Townsville or South Wellesley Islands?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Wellesley Islands has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
